advantages: Manages compiled, non-Python dependencies (CUDA, BLAS) that pip can't.
Prebuilt binaries make GPU/scientific stacks easy to install.
Pins the Python interpreter version per environment.
disadvantages: Heavier install and larger environments than venv.
Dependency solving can be slow (mitigated by mamba/libmamba).
Mixing conda and pip carelessly can corrupt an environment.
Channel/licensing nuances (e.g. defaults vs conda-forge) add complexity.
